exemplar.title: To what extent do some of the current major external partnerships progressions align with Burtâs Beesâ sustainability mission?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear framing of the investigation around sustainability with recurring reference to Burtâs Beesâ strategic pillars
Use of sustainability terminology (circular economy, TBL, CSR) throughout analysis sections
weaknesses: Underdeveloped dedicated definition section on sustainability
Integration in methodology and bibliography sections remains superficial
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student demonstrates a strong understanding of sustainability and applies it consistently in the main analysis, the dedicated concept section is underdeveloped and integration is uneven in some subsections. This aligns most closely with a Band 3 performance.

strengths: Most sources are directly connected to the investigation and supply useful quantitative data
weaknesses: Appendix lists more than five documents, exceeding IA requirements
Limited use of academic or independent third-party studies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Exceeding the maximum number of documents automatically places this strand in the lowest band, despite the relevance and depth of the chosen sources.

strengths: Selection of recognized frameworks (TBL, Porterâs, circular model) aligned to the research question
Coherent application of tools in main body
weaknesses: Application occasionally lapses into description without explicit linkage to the research question
Methodology lacks detail on how frameworks will be applied
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs choice of tools is adequate, but inconsistent depth of application and insufficient methodological detail correspond to a Band 2 performance.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of quantitative indicators to support claims (e.g., waste removed, reduction percentages)
Some linkage of data to organizational goals
weaknesses: Analysis uneven in critical appraisal, often reiterating company claims
Limited triangulation of data and occasional confusion in figures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis shows more than superficial engagement but falls short of sustained, critical evaluation. The mixed depth and redundancy best fit Band 2.

strengths: Conclusions clearly consistent with presented evidence
Explicitly answers the research question âto what extentâ
weaknesses: Omission of study limitations and future research directions
Restatement of results without deeper critical reflection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student delivers conclusions fully consistent with evidence and answers the question, but lacks critical reflection on limitations, aligning with a Band 2.

strengths: Logical sectional organization with clear headings and pagination
Wellâstructured table of contents enabling easy navigation
weaknesses: Inconsistent heading levels (e.g., H3 vs H1/H2)
Contents page could use subâlevels or formatting enhancements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: An appropriate structure is maintained throughout, fulfilling Band 2 criteria despite minor formatting inconsistencies.

strengths: All required IA elements are present (research question, word count, citations, bibliography)
Attention to formatting guidelines (word count on title page)
weaknesses: Broken URLs and inconsistent APA formatting in bibliography
Unexplained superscript footnote marker causing reader confusion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fulfills all prescribed elements and demonstrates attention to guidelines, meriting the top band for this strand.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evident: title page, table of contents, methodology, analysis by pillar, conclusion, bibliography.  Headings and pagination make navigation easy, so an **appropriate structure** is maintained throughoutâBand 2.

Most documents originate from company reports or reputable trade articles and are clearly connected to the research focus (e.g., Clorox IAR 2024, Waste360 article).  They supply useful statistics (pounds of waste removed, % virgin-material reduction) that feed directly into the discussion, showing **relevance and sufficient depth**.  Nevertheless, academic or independent third-party studies are limited, so range and depth stop short of Band 4.
All prescribed IA elements are present: research question, word count, contents page, citations, bibliography and supporting documents list.  Therefore the strand falls in the top presentation band.

The appendix lists far more than five supporting items.  According to the rubric, providing **more than five** documents automatically places the student in Band 1 for this strand, regardless of quality.

Each tool is applied to the research contextâTBL used to frame economic/social/environmental effects, Porter to illustrate differentiation, circular model to describe waste loops.  Application is coherent but often descriptive, and links to the research question occasionally lapse (e.g., Porter analysis veers into global comparisons).  Therefore relevance is **adequate but not consistently convincing**âBand 3.

The student cites multiple quantitative indicators (e.g., 44 million lbs plastic, 10 % reduction, 350 workers).  These figures are integrated into the narrative and help substantiate claims, meeting the âadequateâ descriptor.  Data breadth, triangulation and critical appraisal remain limited, preventing a move to âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ selectionâBand 3.

Analysis goes beyond description by linking data to Burtâs Beesâ goals and by offering a brief risk/ recommendation (AI sorting technology).  Nonetheless, argumentation is uneven and sometimes reiterates company claims without critique, so quality is better than limited but not âmostly effectiveâ.  Band 3 accurately captures **some analysis of the research question**.

The student explicitly answers âto what extentâ by stating that partnerships align âto a notable extentâ and summarises how each pillar is met.  This directly addresses the RQ, matching Band 3.
The student selects three recognised frameworks (Circular Model, TBL, Porterâs Generic Strategies).  This is an **adequate** though not extensive tool kit, aligning with Band 3.
The concluding section restates quantitative evidence (19 % reduction, 77 % women inclusion) and aligns these with earlier data, showing **full consistency** with presented evidence.  Hence Band 3.

The analysis is centred on Burtâs Bees, referencing specific corporate targets, pillars and quantitative progress data drawn from Cloroxâs annual report.  Each tool (TBL, Porter, circular model) is applied directly to Burtâs Bees and its two named partners.  However, some commentary is still descriptive and a few generic sustainability assertions appear (e.g., âAI-powered sortingâ suggestion) without explicit firm-specific linkage.  This reflects **strong analysis with partial, though not complete, integration**âBand 4.

The student consistently frames the investigation around the IB key concept of sustainability, defines it clearly through Burtâs Beesâ two strategic pillars and returns to the concept when discussing each partnership.  Sustainability language (circular economy, TBL, CSR) is woven into both analytical sections, demonstrating solid understanding.  Integration is noticeable in main body paragraphs but not fully sustained in every subsection (e.g., methodology and bibliography sections are largely detached from the concept).  Hence the work shows a **strong but not yet seamless** integrationâbest matched to Band 4.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and directly invokes the IB key concept of sustainability, setting a strong analytical focus for the investigation.
Including the word count on the title page fulfills presentation requirements, demonstrating attention to IA formatting guidelines.
The title page image lacks a descriptive caption or source. Add a brief label to clarify its relevance to Burtâs Bees and sustainability.
The table of contents is well organized, showing appropriate sectional structure and clear navigation throughout the IA.
Consider adding subâlevels or leader dots for each entry in the contents list to improve readability and visual consistency.
The TBL section repeats Clorox report data twice, which dilutes the narrative flow. Streamline the text to avoid redundancy.
The figures â44 millionâ vs. â4.4 millionâ pounds of waste are confusing. Clarify units and context to avoid misinterpretation.
The recommendation to use AI-powered sorting is generic. Tie it more closely to BBâs operational capabilities and risk profile.
The discussion of Coliba would benefit from hard social impact metrics (e.g., income increases, health outcomes) to support claims of community resilience.
The introduction concisely states Burtâs Beesâ sustainability targets with quantitative context, demonstrating clear integration of the sustainability concept.
The âConcept of Sustainabilityâ heading is present but the section itself is underdeveloped. Expand with a concise definition and key dimensions (economic, social, environmental).
The phrase âBB being a part of the CSR communityâ is vague. Clarify how CSR frameworks relate specifically to sustainability rather than relying on shorthand.
The superscript footnote marker â\overline{3}â is unexplained. Add a footnote key or remove it to avoid reader confusion.
The methodology description lacks detail on data selection and analytic procedures. Specify how sources were chosen and how the frameworks will be applied.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Excellent identification of TBL, Porterâs Generic Strategies, and the circular model as analytic tools aligned to the research question.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The âObjective & Methodologyâ heading is clear and helps orient the reader to the research scope.
The citation âBothwell, 2022â conflicts with reference listing of 2023. Verify and correct the publication year for consistency.
The circular model description is largely descriptive. Deepen analysis by linking specific project outcomes to each element of the model.
The TBL subheading uses an h3 tag, unlike earlier h1/h2 headings. Standardize heading levels for consistent structure.
The Porterâs Generic Strategies section is rich in detail but remains descriptive. Explicitly tie differentiation points back to the research question.
The conclusion restates results but omits study limitations and future research directions. Include these to strengthen critical evaluation.
The evidence from Cloroxâs 2024 report is well integrated and supports the conclusion effectively.
Several web citations in the bibliography break URLs across lines and lack consistent APA formatting. Review each reference for style compliance.
The appendix lists more than five supporting documents, exceeding the allowed range. Reduce or consolidate to meet IA requirements.
A number of sources are nonâacademic (e.g., ABC News). Incorporate peer-reviewed studies or industry reports for greater depth.
The citation âHome â repurpose globalâ lacks a publication date and author. Complete missing fields to ensure full traceability.
